# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pandas.core.groupby.base as base

def test_groupbymixin_docstrings_are_multiline_triple_quoted__a1():
    """
    The review change is about docstring *formatting* (single-line -> multiline).
    That isn't observable via inspect.getdoc() (it normalizes whitespace), so we
    assert on the raw __doc__ strings.

    Multiline triple-quoted docstrings in this style begin with a newline and
    include at least one additional newline in the body/indentation.
    """
    raw_cls_doc = base.GroupByMixin.__doc__
    raw_dispatch_doc = base.GroupByMixin._dispatch.__doc__
    assert isinstance(raw_cls_doc, str) and isinstance(raw_dispatch_doc, str), 'Sanity check: expected __doc__ to be present as strings.'

def test_groupbymixin_docstrings_are_multiline_triple_quoted__a2():
    """
    The review change is about docstring *formatting* (single-line -> multiline).
    That isn't observable via inspect.getdoc() (it normalizes whitespace), so we
    assert on the raw __doc__ strings.

    Multiline triple-quoted docstrings in this style begin with a newline and
    include at least one additional newline in the body/indentation.
    """
    raw_cls_doc = base.GroupByMixin.__doc__
    raw_dispatch_doc = base.GroupByMixin._dispatch.__doc__
    assert raw_cls_doc.startswith('\n') and '\n' in raw_cls_doc[1:], 'Expected GroupByMixin docstring to be in multiline triple-quote style (raw __doc__ should start with a newline and contain a subsequent newline).'

def test_groupbymixin_docstrings_are_multiline_triple_quoted__a3():
    """
    The review change is about docstring *formatting* (single-line -> multiline).
    That isn't observable via inspect.getdoc() (it normalizes whitespace), so we
    assert on the raw __doc__ strings.

    Multiline triple-quoted docstrings in this style begin with a newline and
    include at least one additional newline in the body/indentation.
    """
    raw_cls_doc = base.GroupByMixin.__doc__
    raw_dispatch_doc = base.GroupByMixin._dispatch.__doc__
    assert raw_dispatch_doc.startswith('\n') and '\n' in raw_dispatch_doc[1:], 'Expected GroupByMixin._dispatch docstring to be in multiline triple-quote style (raw __doc__ should start with a newline and contain a subsequent newline).'

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pandas as pd
from pandas.core.generic import _shared_docs

def test_shared_docs_melt_docstring_does_not_start_with_quotes__a1():
    """
    The shared docstring for melt should not start with quotes.

    This matches the review request to "simply remove the quotes" and ensures
    docstring validation tools don't see a leading quote character.
    """
    doc = _shared_docs['melt']
    assert isinstance(doc, str) and doc, "Expected pandas.core.generic._shared_docs['melt'] to be a non-empty string"

def test_shared_docs_melt_docstring_does_not_start_with_quotes__a2():
    """
    The shared docstring for melt should not start with quotes.

    This matches the review request to "simply remove the quotes" and ensures
    docstring validation tools don't see a leading quote character.
    """
    doc = _shared_docs['melt']
    stripped = doc.lstrip()
    assert not stripped.startswith(("'", '"')), f"""Expected pandas.core.generic._shared_docs['melt'] to not start with a quote after leading whitespace is stripped; quotes should be removed (e.g. start with 'Unpivots ...' rather than "'Unpivots' ..."). Got leading snippet: {stripped[:30]!r}"""

def test_shared_docs_melt_docstring_does_not_start_with_quotes__a3():
    """
    The shared docstring for melt should not start with quotes.

    This matches the review request to "simply remove the quotes" and ensures
    docstring validation tools don't see a leading quote character.
    """
    doc = _shared_docs['melt']
    stripped = doc.lstrip()
    assert stripped.startswith('Unpivots'), f"Expected melt shared docstring to start with 'Unpivots' once quotes are removed; got leading snippet: {stripped[:30]!r}"

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_styler_clear_docstring_reset_has_no_quotes__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/pandas/io/formats/style.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def clear\\(self\\):\\n(?P<body>(?:[ \\t].*\\n)+)', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    assert m is not None, 'Expected to find `def clear(self):` in style.py'

def test_styler_clear_docstring_reset_has_no_quotes__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/pandas/io/formats/style.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def clear\\(self\\):\\n(?P<body>(?:[ \\t].*\\n)+)', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    body = m.group('body')
    assert "'Reset' the styler" not in body, "Styler.clear docstring should not quote the word Reset (expect `Reset the styler ...`, not `'Reset' the styler ...`)."

def test_styler_clear_docstring_reset_has_no_quotes__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/pandas/io/formats/style.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def clear\\(self\\):\\n(?P<body>(?:[ \\t].*\\n)+)', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    body = m.group('body')
    assert 'Reset the styler, removing any previously applied styles.' in body, 'Expected Styler.clear docstring line: `Reset the styler, removing any previously applied styles.`'
